---
title: "MBYEXA001_TSHPAT016_Assignment3"
author: "Exauce Mbuyi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(tidyverse)
library(GGally)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
load("airbnbListings.RData")
```

\section{Data Wrangling & Part One}

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# First remove all missing valued objects:
clean_data <- na.omit(Airbnb_Excel_Lab_AAMB_Co)

# Variable renaming:
clean_data <- clean_data %>% 
  rename(
    ward_level = Neighbourhood_Cleansed,
    property_type = `Property_Type_Proper & Trim`,
    host_name = `Host_Name_Trimmed & Proper`,
    room_type = `Room_Type_Proper & Trim`,
    neighbourhood = `Neighbourhood_Proper & Trim`
         )

# Checking for variables with no variability in them:
length(unique(clean_data$room_type))
length(unique(clean_data$property_type))
length(unique(clean_data$host_name))
length(unique(clean_data$neighbourhood))

# Performing PCA for dimension reduction:
numeric_cols <- sapply(clean_data, is.numeric)
numeric_data <- clean_data[, numeric_cols] # Taking only the numerical variables

PCA_results <- prcomp(numeric_data, center = T, scale. = T)

# Scree plot:
plot(PCA_results, type = 'l', main = "Scree Plot") 

# Obtain first 5 loading vectors:
loadings_mat <- PCA_results$rotation[ , 1:4]

# Creating new variables:
availability <- PCA_results$x[ , 1]
review_score <- PCA_results$x[ , 2]
listing_size <- PCA_results$x[ , 3]
num_of_reviews <- PCA_results$x[ , 4]
#identifier <- PCA_results$x[ , 5]

# scatter plot matrix:
pairs(PCA_results$x[, 1:4], 
      col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.2),
      pch = 16, 
      main = "Scatterplot Matrix of Top 4 PCs")

# EDA for Ward variable:
PC_Summary <- data.frame(
  PCA_results$x[ , 1:4],
  Ward = clean_data$ward_level
)

Ward_averages <- PC_Summary %>% 
  group_by(Ward) %>% 
  summarise(across(starts_with("PC"), mean, .names = "avg_col{.col}"))

# EDA for the room type:
PC_Summary$RoomType <- clean_data$room_type

ggpairs(PC_Summary, 
        columns = 1:4, 
        aes(color = RoomType, alpha = 0.4)) +
  theme_minimal()

# EDA for property type:
clean_data <- clean_data %>%
  mutate(property_category = case_when(
    grepl("^entire", tolower(property_type))  ~ "Entire Unit",
    grepl("^private", tolower(property_type)) ~ "Private Room",
    TRUE                                      ~ "Others"
  ))

PC_Summary$property_category <- clean_data$property_category

# EDA for property category themes:
eda_theme_df <- data.frame(
  availability   = availability,
  review_score   = review_score,
  listing_size   = listing_size,
  num_of_reviews = num_of_reviews,
  property_category = clean_data$property_category
)

eda_theme_long <- eda_theme_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -property_category, 
    names_to = "Theme", 
    values_to = "Score"
  )

# Generate the faceted boxplot with constrained Y-axis
ggplot(eda_theme_long, aes(x = property_category, y = Score, fill = property_category)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) + 
  facet_wrap(~Theme, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-5, 5)) + # This sets the view to [-1, 1]
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Airbnb Theme Distributions by Property Category",
    subtitle = "Analysis of Principal Component Scores (Focus Range)",
    y = "PC Score (Standard Deviations)", 
    x = "Category",
    fill = "Property Type"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 

# EDA on the host_name variable:
host_freq_table <- clean_data %>%
  count(host_name, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    relative_freq = n / sum(n),
    percentage = relative_freq * 100
  )

head(host_freq_table, 10)

# EDA on Neighbourhood:
luxury_labels <- clean_data %>%
  group_by(neighbourhood) %>%
  summarise(
    lat = mean(Latitude),
    long = mean(Longitude),
    avg_price = mean(Price)
  ) %>%
  filter(avg_price > 1000) %>%

  mutate(short_name = gsub(",.*", "", neighbourhood))


ggplot(clean_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Price), alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8) + 
  scale_color_viridis(option = "magma", trans = "log10", 
                      labels = scales::dollar_format(prefix = "R")) +
  geom_text(data = luxury_labels, 
            aes(x = long, y = lat, label = short_name), # Using short_name
            size = 3, 
            check_overlap = TRUE, 
            fontface = "bold",
            color = "black",
            vjust = -0.5) + 
  theme_minimal() +

  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(18.2, 19.1), ylim = c(-34.3, -33.5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Luxury Airbnb Hubs (Avg > R1,000)",
    subtitle = "Standardized Labels for Cape Town Clusters",
    color = "Price per Night"
  )

clean_data_copy <- clean_data
```

\section{Part Two}

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

```


\section{Part Three}

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

```
